name: Ba Xian
terroir: High elevation Wu Dong Shan, mineral-rich volcanic soil
flavor_profile: A deeply floral Dan Cong with an orchid-magnolia character that avoids being perfumy. Opens with peach blossom and raw honey, develops into woodsy chestnut notes by the third infusion. Pronounced returning sweetness with kumquat and apricot undertones.

brewing.rinse_hint: Quick 5-second rinse to open the tightly rolled leaves.
brewing.tips: Fill the gaiwan about two-thirds full with leaf. Dan Cong is sensitive to oversteeping — keep early infusions fast to avoid bitterness.
